title: East Timor 1999: Crimes Against Humanity? A Report Commissioned by the United Nations Office of the High Commissioner for Human Rights
strengths: High-value expert synthesis tied to the UN/CAVR documentary record.
limitations: Focused on civilian victimization; does not provide a comparable combatant-victim death series for DR.
notes: Paraphrase: between early January and late October 1999, at least 1,200 civilians and perhaps as many as 1,500 were killed.

title: East Timor: militias, military and the police—responsibility for crimes against humanity
strengths: Strong contemporaneous documentation of militia-security force cooperation, security-force presence, and mass displacement.
limitations: Does not solve combatant-victim denominator or combatant-victim death estimation.
notes: Paraphrase: abuses were committed by militias, TNI, and some Indonesian police; by ballot day there were about 8,000 police and 15,000 TNI in East Timor; more than 200,000 people fled or were forcibly relocated.

title: Timor-Leste Armed Violence Assessment issue brief
strengths: Useful independent cross-check on the scale and perpetrators of violence around the ballot.
limitations: Gives a rounded total rather than a status-separated victim line-list.
notes: Paraphrase: roughly 1,500 people were killed during the August 1999 ballot, primarily by Indonesian security forces and militias.

title: Operation Stabilise and UNTAET lessons study
strengths: Important for showing why a combatant-victim numerator is hard: FALINTIL largely remained in cantonment rather than fighting openly during the militia rampage.
limitations: Still not a direct casualty count.
notes: Paraphrase: before and during the consultation, FALINTIL forces remained in cantonment areas and largely refrained from engaging the militias.
